text: One common way to find bugs is to use simple logging. By adding print statements or writing messages to a log file, you can see what your code is doing at each step. Another helpful tool is a debugger, which lets you pause your program and inspect variables. However, the most important thing is a systematic approach. Instead of guessing, change one thing at a time and test. This helps you pinpoint the exact source of a bug, making debugging much faster and less frustrating.
